beginner.question: What is the <q> element used for?
beginner.answer: The <q> element is used for short inline quotations. Browsers automatically add quotation marks around the content.

beginner.question: What is the <blockquote> element used for?
beginner.answer: <blockquote> is used for longer block-level quotations. It is typically indented and can contain multiple paragraphs.

beginner.question: What is the difference between <q> and <blockquote>?
beginner.answer: <q> is an inline element for short quotes (browser adds quotes). <blockquote> is a block-level element for long quotes (browser adds indentation).

beginner.question: What is the <cite> element used for?
beginner.answer: <cite> marks the title of a creative work such as books, articles, movies, or songs. It is typically rendered in italic.

beginner.question: What is the <abbr> element used for?
beginner.answer: <abbr> marks up abbreviations. The title attribute can provide the full expansion, shown as a tooltip on hover.

beginner.question: What is the <address> element used for?
beginner.answer: <address> marks up contact information for the page or article author, including email, phone, and physical address.

beginner.question: What does the cite attribute do on <blockquote>?
beginner.answer: The cite attribute specifies the URL of the source from which the quotation is taken. It is not visible by default.

beginner.question: How are nested <q> elements displayed?
beginner.answer: Browsers automatically alternate between quotation mark styles for nested <q> elements (e.g., double, then single).

beginner.question: What is the default styling of <blockquote>?
beginner.answer: <blockquote> is typically rendered with left and right indentation (about 40px margin) by default.

beginner.question: What should you NOT use <cite> for?
beginner.answer: In HTML5, <cite> should not be used for people's names. It is restricted to titles of creative works.

intermediate.question: How does the cite attribute differ between <q> and <blockquote>?
intermediate.answer: Both accept a cite attribute for the source URL, but it affects semantics the same way — it is not rendered visually but can be accessed programmatically.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between <cite> and a regular italic style?
intermediate.answer: <cite> has semantic meaning — it identifies the title of a work. Regular italic (<i> or CSS font-style) has no semantic meaning.

intermediate.question: How do you properly attribute a quote within a <blockquote>?
intermediate.answer:
Use a

for the quote text and a for the source attribution inside the

. The cite attribute on
provides the URL.

intermediate.question: What is the difference between <abbr> and <acronym>?
intermediate.answer: <abbr> is for all abbreviations. <acronym> was for pronounceable acronyms but is deprecated in HTML5. Use <abbr> for everything.

intermediate.question: How does the <address> element affect the document outline?
intermediate.answer: <address> does not affect the document outline. It provides contact info for the nearest <article> or <body> ancestor.

intermediate.question: What is a pull quote and how is it marked up semantically?
intermediate.answer: A pull quote is a key phrase repeated from the article in a sidebar. Use <aside> containing a <blockquote> for semantic markup.

intermediate.question: How do you style a <blockquote> with CSS for a magazine look?
intermediate.answer: Use border-left, background color, larger font-size, italic style, and padding. Example: blockquote { border-left: 4px solid #ccc; font-style: italic; }

intermediate.question: What quote element should be used for dialogue in a story?
intermediate.answer: For dialogue, use <q> for short exchanges within paragraphs. For longer dialogue blocks, use <blockquote>.

intermediate.question: How can you indicate the language of a quoted passage?
intermediate.answer: Use the lang attribute on the specific element: <q lang='fr'>Ceci est une citation</q>.

intermediate.question: What is the relationship between <blockquote> and the CSS quotes property?
intermediate.answer: The CSS quotes property controls the quotation mark characters used for <q> elements, allowing customization of the quotation style.

advanced.question: How does the <q> element interact with the CSS quotes property?
advanced.answer: The quotes property defines the quotation mark pairs. <q> elements use these defined characters automatically, supporting nested levels and different languages.

advanced.question: What is the accessibility tree mapping for <blockquote> and <q>?
advanced.answer: <blockquote> maps to the 'blockquote' ARIA role. <q> maps to the 'quote' role. Screen readers may announce 'quote' before reading the content.

advanced.question: How does the <cite> element interact with the accessibility tree?
advanced.answer: <cite> has no specific ARIA role. It is typically exposed as a generic element with italic styling. Screen readers do not announce it specially.

advanced.question: What is the correct usage of <address> in relation to the document's author?
advanced.answer: <address> should contain contact info for the document's author, not general business addresses. It belongs in <footer> or near the author's bio.

advanced.question: How does the HTML spec define the content model for <blockquote>?
advanced.answer: <blockquote> accepts flow content (headings, paragraphs, lists, etc.). This means you can put complex content inside a blockquote.

advanced.question: What is the semantic way to mark up a quote that has been translated?
advanced.answer:
Use the original quote in a
with the lang attribute set to the original language, followed by a

with the translation.

advanced.question: How do you mark up a conversation between two people semantically?
advanced.answer:
Use

elements with for each line of dialogue, or use a definition list (

) with
for speaker names and
for their speech.

advanced.question: What is the difference between the cite attribute and the <cite> element?
advanced.answer: The cite attribute provides a machine-readable URL for the source. The <cite> element provides a human-readable citation label.

advanced.question: How does the <bdo> element differ from <bdi> in bidirectional text scenarios?
advanced.answer: <bdo> overrides the direction of its content. <bdi> isolates its content from surrounding directionality without overriding.

advanced.question: What is the correct way to handle quotation marks across different languages with CSS?
advanced.answer: Use the CSS quotes property with the appropriate pair for each language, combined with the lang attribute on the HTML element to switch quotes automatically.
